title: Dr. Brown's Anti-Colic Options+ Narrow Baby Bottle, 8oz/250ml, with Level 1 Slow Flow Teat, 3-Pack
features: ANTI-COLIC BABY BOTTLE. Anti-Colic internal vent system is clinically proven to reduce colic. Decreases spit-up, burping and wind.
CONSISTENT FLOW RATE. Vacuum-free feeding is closest to breastfeeding and Dr. Brown’s silicone teats provide a consistent flow so babies can feed at their own pace.
PRESERVES NUTRIENTS. The anti-colic vent system is proven to help preserve bottle milk nutrients and aids in digestion for a better night’s sleep.

BOTTLE GROWS WITH BABY. Dr. Brown’s Natural Flow Anti-Colic Options+ baby bottle works best with the internal vent system in place but can be removed as baby’s feeding develops – no need to introduce a different bottle.
DISHWASHER SAFE. Safe for use in dishwasher (top rack only), electric and microwave sterilisers or boiling water. BPA Free.
description: Your options have never been better with Dr. Brown’s Natural Flow Anti-Colic Options+ Narrow Baby Bottle. The anti-colic internal vent system is clinically proven to reduce colic. Dr. Brown’ bottles provide a more comfortable feeding experience for baby by decreasing spit-up, burping and wind. This vacuum-free feeding is closest to breastfeeding and Dr. Brown’s silicone teats provide a consistent flow so baby can feed at their own pace. The breast-like teat shape encourages a proper latch for a more natural bottle feeding experience and eases the transition from breast to bottle and back again. Dr. Brown’s vent system is proven to help preserve bottle milk nutrients and aids in digestion for a better night’s sleep. The silicone teat, together with the internal vent system, offer a reliable and trusted bottle feeding experience that makes Dr. Brown’s the #1 Healthcare Professional Recommended Baby Bottle in the U.K.* Made to grow with baby, our bottle is designed to nourish through all of baby’s feeding stages. Safe for use in dishwasher (top rack only), electric and microwave sterilisers or boiling water. BPA Free. *Mustard research 2017
